title: hieracium sabaudum subsp. virescens
intro: Hieracium sabaudum subsp. virescens, also known as hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to certain regions of Europe. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is also said to have health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Hieracium sabaudum subsp. virescens are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
